questions.text: Which part of the plant is responsible for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll which captures sunlight energy for photosynthesis. They are the primary food-making organs of plants.

questions.text: Plants take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen during photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil, using sunlight to produce glucose and oxygen.

questions.text: What is the green pigment in plants called?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment found in chloroplasts of plant cells. It absorbs light energy needed for photosynthesis.

questions.text: The process by which plants make their own food is called ________.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process where plants use sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water to create glucose (food) and oxygen.
